humanities_role_explanation: Evaluates models on the domain of art history, criticism, and aesthetics using expert-annotated critiques of historical, multicultural artworks across eight cultural traditions.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Multimodal dataset pairing high-resolution artwork images from diverse cultures with expert-written bilingual (Chinese and English) art critiques.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Evaluates generative cultural understanding using a five-layer ascending framework: L1 (visual perception), L2 (technical analysis), L3 (cultural symbolism), L4 (historical context), and L5 (philosophical aesthetics).

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Specialized domain benchmark designed to evaluate multicultural art critique and deep cultural understanding.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates advanced vision-language models including GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Claude 3.5 Opus, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Qwen3-VL-235B, GLM-4V-Flash, and DeepSeek-VL2.

llm_is_primary_system.note: The primary focus is evaluating the generative reasoning capabilities of VLMs on structured cultural interpretation tasks.

arts_humanities_data.note: Focuses on Visual Arts, with deep intersections in History, Philosophy (indigenous aesthetics), and Religious Studies (e.g., Buddhist iconography).

quantitative_metrics.note: Introduces Dimension Coverage Rate (DCR) via keyword matching, validated by embedding similarity and NLI entailment.

published_after_march_2023.note: Published as a preprint in February 2026.

english_language.note: The paper and critiques are in English, with bilingual Chinese text included.

summary: VULCA-BENCH is a multicultural art-critique benchmark containing 7,410 image-critique pairs across eight traditions, designed to evaluate the depth of cultural understanding in Vision-Language Models. By adopting a five-layer hierarchical framework (from basic visual perception to philosophical aesthetics) and enforcing a Cultural Symmetry Principle, it provides a rigorous basis for evaluating AI systems on complex, domain-expert analytical reasoning.
key_findings: State-of-the-art VLMs suffer a dramatic 25-40 percentage-point performance drop when transitioning from visual/technical perception (L1-L2) to cultural/philosophical interpretation (L3-L5). Error analyses indicate models rely on superficial terminology, exhibit historical anachronisms, and conflate cultural traditions. Additionally, naive few-shot prompting degrades performance for most models.
review_notes: Resolved minor discrepancies. Adopted a merged list of analytical tasks utilizing the paper's L1-L5 schema naming. Kept History in the ASJC areas since art historical attribution and context analysis are core to L4. Set primary_evaluation_setting to 'mix' since both zero-shot and few-shot (1-shot, 3-shot) experiments are conducted and explicitly compared in the study.
